recommendation: Implement a small, explicit, versioned, standard-library-only module I/O envelope contract layer before any real module execution.
rationale: The module manifest already acts as the capability declaration layer, so a broad capability registry would duplicate manifest/profile semantics and risk drift.
The missing next contract is the normalized object future modules would receive and the constrained object they would eventually return.
This can be implemented fully offline without importing or executing module code.
acceptance_criteria: Add versioned module_input/1.0 and module_result/1.0 contracts.
P2-10 allows only dry-run, non-target-touching, no-network, no-finding/evidence contracts.
Runner remains unable to execute or import module implementation code.
Tests reject malformed, non-dry-run, target-touching, network, unsafe output, path traversal, duplicate/unknown, and completed-result cases.
Docs explicitly state the safety boundary and non-goals.
Full scripts suite, Hermes review, and independent implementation review pass.
what_not_to_implement: Do not implement real module execution or check.py loading/importing.
Do not add subprocess execution for modules or scanners.
Do not add network probes, callbacks, target-touching behavior, or active/live profiles.
Do not emit real findings/evidence or generate reports.
Do not create a broad capability registry duplicating manifest/profile fields.
Do not loosen existing manifest/profile/run validators.
